task_completion.description: Open the InventoryManager project in Android Studio
task_completion.visual_evidence: Android Studio is open and the Project tool window shows the 'InventoryManager' project structure.

task_completion.description: Add the kotlin-parcelize plugin to the app's build.gradle.kts
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor shows 'app/build.gradle.kts' with 'id("kotlin-parcelize")' added to the 'plugins' block.

task_completion.description: Sync the project with Gradle files
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Gradle sync is completed successfully (e.g., the 'Sync Now' banner is gone, or the Build/Sync tool window shows a successful sync message).

task_completion.description: Annotate the Item class with @Parcelize and implement the Parcelable interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor shows 'Item.kt' where the 'Item' data class is annotated with '@Parcelize' and implements the interface (e.g., 'data class Item(...) : Parcelable').
The Build tool window at the bottom of Android Studio is open and displays a successful build message (e.g., 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' or a green checkmark indicating the build finished without errors).

integrity.description: The agent used the Android Studio GUI to edit files and trigger the build
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Android Studio editor and tool windows being used, rather than relying solely on terminal commands like 'sed' or './gradlew build'.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the code and IDE
integrity.visual_evidence: Cursor movements, typing, and menu navigations are visible in the IDE, rather than a single paste of the final code.
The build output appears in the actual Android Studio Build tool window with standard Gradle logs, not in a mocked text file or terminal echo.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/InventoryManager. The 'Item' data class is in 'app/src/main/java/com/example/inventory/model/Item.kt'. The 'app/build.gradle.kts' file initially lacks the 'kotlin-parcelize' plugin. To fix the compilation error, the agent must: 1) Add 'id("kotlin-parcelize")' to the plugins block in 'app/build.gradle.kts', 2) Add the '@Parcelize' annotation to the 'Item' class, 3) Make the 'Item' class implement 'android.os.Parcelable'. Finally, the project must be built successfully.
